codeReview.summary: Implementation is functionally correct and meets all requirements. The SizeEstimation component displays ~ prefixed estimated size, correctly calculates source_size * compression_ratio * trim_factor, and shows warning when estimated > source. Compression ratios verified against presets.ts (compact=0.25, good=0.40, high=0.65, custom=0.40). Component is integrated into App.tsx after QualityPreset. Unit tests exist but two warning-related tests use placeholder assertions.

issues.description: Test 'shows warning when estimated > source size (high preset on compressible source)' uses placeholder `expect(true).toBe(true)` instead of actual test logic. The worker correctly identified in comments that with compression ratios < 1, the warning cannot mathematically trigger in normal usage. The test should either test the logic path directly (via mocking/extreme values) or be marked as skipped with an explanation.

Test 'shows warning when trim factor would make output larger than source (theoretical case)' uses placeholder `expect(true).toBe(true)`. Same issue as above - the warning logic is correctly implemented but not actually exercised by the test.

The `.size::before { content: ''; }` rule is empty and serves no purpose since the ~ prefix is already in the JSX as `~{formatSize(estimatedSize)}`. This is harmless but indicates possible confusion about CSS-based tilde styling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill explicitly requires agent-browser invocation for manual verification of the size-estimation-component. The skill states: 'Verify: Select High preset on compressible source, warning appears; Select Compact, no warning.' The worker's transcript shows no agent-browser calls and the handoff's interactiveChecks array is empty.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Frontend-worker SKILL.md for size-estimation-component section. Handoff verification section shows empty interactiveChecks array. Transcript skeleton shows no agent-browser tool invocations.

summary: SizeEstimation component is correctly implemented with ~ prefix, proper compression ratio calculations (compact=0.25, good=0.40, high=0.65, custom=0.40), trim factor support, and warning display logic. Component integrated into App.tsx after QualityPreset. Unit tests exist but two warning-related tests are placeholders (the worker noted the mathematical impossibility of warning triggering with current ratios). No interactive verification via agent-browser was performed per skill procedure.
